title: ophrys × domus-maria
intro: Ophrys × domus-maria, also known as the bee orchid, is a hybrid species of orchid that is a cross between Ophrys apifera and Ophrys sphegodes. This plant is primarily found in the Mediterranean region and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The extracts from the Ophrys × domus-maria have been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and preventing infections. Herbal preparations of the Ophrys × domus-maria can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and promote overall health.
